fix: prevent API queries from firing before authentication

Split App into an auth gate component and AuthenticatedApp. Data queries
(accounts, payees, categories, etc.) now only mount after auth is confirmed,
eliminating the race condition that caused "Unauthorized" errors on first login.

feat(grpc-removal): delete proto/eth/v1 package and proto/migration (Phase 6.4) (#35)

Remove the entire proto/eth/v1 directory and all references across the
codebase. Event types (EventHead, EventFinalizedCheckpoint, EventChainReorg)
are replaced with plain Go structs in beacon-chain/core/feed/state.
ValidatorStatus enum replaced with consensus-types/validator.StatusFromString.

direct v1alpha1 enum usage. V1 hydrate helpers and migration functions deleted.

point to their actual locations in proto/prysm/v1alpha1/:

- AttestationData, Checkpoint → attestation.proto
- BeaconBlock, BeaconBlockBody, AttesterSlashing → beacon_block.proto
- BeaconBlockHeader, ProposerSlashing, Deposit, VoluntaryExit → beacon_core_types.proto

commit.message:
Samsung upload: tqdm progress, faster uploads, reboot retry (#119)

* Samsung upload: tqdm progress bar, user-only art count in logs

- Replace silent upload loop with tqdm progress bar showing current file
- Log messages now report user-uploaded image count (MY_F) not total art
- Per-file upload success/recovery logged at debug level

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* send+poll upload: bypass WebSocket response timeout

Upload images via direct TCP send, then poll art list for new content_id
instead of waiting for the library's `image_added` WebSocket response
(which races/times out on large batches — NickWaterton/samsung-tv-ws-api#10).

- _send_image_data: handshake + TCP chunk transfer, returns after send
- _poll_for_new_upload: polls art list every 3s up to 60s for new ID
- upload_image: uses send+poll when known_ids provided (batch mode)
- 10s sleep between uploads, reboot retries 5x @ 30s fixed wait

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Revert send+poll, remove upload retry to fix ~80s/img bottleneck

The tenacity retry on upload_image was re-uploading the same image 5x
on timeout (art WebSocket timeout is only 5s), each re-upload succeeding
on TV but timing out again. Removing retry + relying on caller's
_check_for_new_upload cuts per-image time from ~80s to ~40s.

Send+poll approach reverted: bypassing wait_for_response("image_added")
corrupts the TV's art WebSocket state, causing session disconnects.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix API data field mapping and GitHub Actions workflow path

- Add normalizeNotice() to convert API fields (bidNtceNm, ntceInsttNm, etc.)
  to render format (name, org, type, bidEnd, etc.)
- Use _relevance from Python script directly in getRelevance()
- Move daily_fetch.yml to .github/workflows/ so GitHub Actions picks it up

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: bucket date always starts from Today view date; swipe stays in bucket

Issue 1: BucketDateNav.init() was restoring a stale localStorage date per
bucket, causing different buckets to open on different dates (yesterday,
Feb 28, etc.). Now always initializes to whatever date the main Today tab
is currently showing (viewDate()), so if you're viewing Mar 8, every bucket
opens on Mar 8.

Issue 2: Global initSwipe() in app.js was firing alongside the bucket's own
swipe handler. Both ran on the same gesture — bucket updated BucketDateNav
correctly, but app.js also clicked app-prev-day/app-next-day, triggering a
full Hub re-render that called _cleanupBucketView() and closed the bucket.
Fix: bail out of global swipe handling when .hub-bucket-backbar is present.

commit.message:
fix(pills): correct ai@6 API usage for tool invocations

Three breaking changes from ai@4 → ai@6 that prevented pills from rendering:
- m.toolInvocations doesn't exist → use m.parts filtered for type:'dynamic-tool'
- inv.args doesn't exist → use inv.input (server-side tools = DynamicToolUIPart)
- addToolResult({ toolCallId, result }) → addToolResult({ tool, toolCallId, output })

Backend reverts to native @ai-sdk/google which parses Gemini streaming chunks
correctly (no missing-index validation error unlike the OpenAI-compat wrapper).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: Improve Wikidata QID disambiguation for books and other media

Two complementary fixes to stop the title-search fallback from
matching the wrong Wikidata entity when bridge ID lookup yields nothing.

ISBN urn: prefix stripping
  EPUBs embed ISBNs as URIs (urn:isbn:9780451524935).  Wikidata stores
  bare numbers (9780451524935).  GetBridgeValue now strips the
  "urn:isbn:" prefix before building the SPARQL bridge query so that
  ISBN-based QID resolution can succeed.

Description-scoring disambiguation
  ResolveQidViaSearchAsync now scores all candidates from
  wbsearchentities using their Wikidata description:
    +2  description contains affinity keywords for the media type
        (e.g. "novel", "literary work" for Books)
    -2  description contains rejection keywords
        (e.g. "calendar year", "film" when looking for a Book)
    +1  match type is "label" (primary label, not alias)
  Highest-scoring candidate wins.

Before: "1984" matched Q2432 (calendar year 1984, instance_of=leap year),
        "Dune" matched Q60834962 (2021 film), "The Hobbit" matched Q1187415
        (animated TV film).
After:  all three correctly resolve — Q208460 (Nineteen Eighty-Four),
        Q190192 (1965 Dune novel), Q74287 (Tolkien novel).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Initial project skeleton: Rust workspace with 4 crates

- openra-sim: shared simulation core (zero dependencies)
- openra-data: file format parsers (.orarep, .oramap, SHP, palette)
- openra-wasm: browser replay viewer (WebGL + WASM)
- openra-train: training runtime (128 parallel sims)
- Docker dev environment for building without local Rust install
- Architecture doc covering both requirements (replay + training)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add master data pipeline: extract every training resource, waste nothing

New scripts:
- load_batches.py: Extract 6,395 pairs from 1,210 distill batch files
- skills_to_pairs.py: Convert 22 SKILL.md modules into 734 training pairs
- build_training_dataset.py: Master orchestrator consolidating 9 data sources
  (batches, skills, datasets, replay, training, mined, weakness, self_improve, reasoning)
- run_miner.py: Multi-provider miner runner with scheduling + graceful shutdown

Enhanced:
- regression_eval.py: Auto-triggers weakness_hunter.py on regression failure
  (closes the eval→training feedback loop)

Pipeline produces 9,818 deduplicated, quality-filtered pairs from all sources.
Domain-balanced with 2x Hive oversampling, 45% thinking pairs.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

test: add cross-layer consistency checks for function arg specs (#32)

(interpreter layer), and suggestionTag (TUI layer) at test time.

- Every BuiltinFunction has a matching FunctionSpec and vice versa

commit.message:
Fix non-null literal equality producing wrong IsNotType narrowing

`x == 1` being false doesn't mean x isn't an int (x could be 2). Only
null is a singleton type where equality-being-false proves the variable
isn't that type. Previously all literal comparisons produced IsNotType
in the else-branch, causing spurious `~int` errors in nixpkgs
strings.nix (levenshteinAtMost: chained `k == 1` / `k == 2` checks).

commit.message:
Fix iOS DOM corruption: CSS guardrail + MutationObserver replaces timing-dependent checks (#106)

Previous fixes used useEffect/setTimeout to detect orphaned <main> elements,
but these have a fundamental timing race — the check fires before or after
the corruption is detectable. This new approach uses two layers:

1. CSS `main:has(~ main) { display: none }` hides stale pages instantly
   before paint, with zero JS involvement
2. MutationObserver in ScrollRestorer proactively removes orphaned <main>
   nodes the moment they appear in the DOM

Removes checkDomIntegrity from ServiceWorkerRegistrar since the
MutationObserver now handles it continuously.

fix: campus map tiles not loading when tab starts hidden

The map container initializes inside a CSS `hidden` tab, so Leaflet
can't measure the container size. A single requestAnimationFrame
invalidateSize() wasn't enough. Added a ResizeObserver that calls
invalidateSize() when the container gains dimensions (i.e. when the
tab becomes visible).

Standardize placeholder format to {{PLACEHOLDER}} across codebase (#674)

* Standardize de-identification placeholders to {{PLACEHOLDER}} format

Update all scrub/restore/descrub code to use {{DOUBLE_CURLY}} placeholders
matching the data pipeline conventions (e.g. {{FIRST_NAME}}, {{LAST_NAME}},
{{SCSID}}, {{GPID}}, {{CASEID}}, {{Your Phone Number}}, {{Your Email Address}}).

TypeScript changes:
- scrub_scrub.ts: regex replacement strings now use {{PLACEHOLDER}} format;
  storeIdToPlaceholder mapping converts store_* input IDs to {{}} placeholders
- user_info_storage.ts: scrubPersonalInfo uses {{}} placeholders instead of [];
  restorePersonalInfo matches {{}} regex patterns
- appeal.ts: descrub() handles {{}} placeholders first, then legacy fallbacks

Python changes:
- ml_models.py: system prompts use {{FIRST_NAME}} {{LAST_NAME}} instead of
  YourNameMagic, {{SCSID}} instead of SCSID, {{GPID}} instead of GPID
- common_view_logic.py: sub_in_appeals includes {{}} format entries for
  insurance company, case/claim IDs, names; simplified replacement loop
- prior_auth_utils.py: default placeholders use {{}} format; regex pattern
  matches $var, [var], and {{var}} formats
- test_prior_auth_utils.py: updated assertions to expect {{}} placeholders

https://claude.ai/code/session_01Td8qMDidY4DYcMevYrAhgV

* Fix black formatting in prior_auth_utils.py

* Fix tox test failures: sync Stripe skip, selenium proxy, and PII rehydration

- rest_auth_views.py: Check TESTING env var alongside DEBUG for skip_stripe,
  since Django forces DEBUG=False in tests (fixes 3 sync test failures)
- fhi_selenium_base.py: Inject --no-proxy-server Chrome arg to prevent
  localhost traffic routing through HTTPS proxy (fixes all selenium timeouts)
- prior_auth_utils.py: Add canonical placeholder keys (date_of_birth,
  npi_number, etc.) and fix empty-value fallbacks using `or` operator
- appeal.ts: Convert legacy string replacements to global regex patterns
  to replace all occurrences, not just the first
- user_info_storage.ts: Reorder email scrub before name replacement to
  prevent email corruption; add legacy placeholder restores
- test_selenium_pii_rehydration.py: Navigate to /scan instead of / to
  obtain CSRF token from form; simplify CSRF token acquisition
